title: Orient Express — the last direct run

intro.title: The Last Direct Paris–Istanbul
intro.body: For most of a century a traveller could board a single train in Paris and step down at the edge of the Bosphorus. On a spring evening the last direct service pulled out of the Gare de Lyon for Istanbul, and the through-carriage that had carried diplomats, spies and novelists finally uncoupled from the timetable. What remained afterward was the name, and the memory of a continent crossed in one unbroken run.

stops.name: Paris (Gare de Lyon)
stops.note: The through-carriage waits at the far end of the platform, its enamel board still lettered for a city three days east. The buffet clock reads a quarter to departure.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Evening under the Gare de Lyon train-shed, a single blue sleeping-car with a brass ISTANBUL board, porters loading leather cases, gold-lit clock tower beyond the glass.

stops.name: Milan
stops.note: Dawn over the Lombard plain, the Alps already behind. A change of engines under the vast iron canopy while the corridor windows fog with coffee steam.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Milano Centrale's monumental steel-and-stone train hall at morning, an express drawn up alongside, travellers stretching on the platform.

stops.name: Venice (Santa Lucia)
stops.note: The line runs out across the lagoon causeway and the station doors open straight onto water. A few minutes of vaporetto bells, then the train draws east again toward the mountains.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Santa Lucia station steps meeting the Grand Canal, a waiting vaporetto, the domes of the city low on the far bank under a pale sky.

stops.name: Belgrade
stops.note: A long midnight halt where the platforms smell of coal and lime blossom. Frontier officials work the corridor by torchlight; the carriage is shunted to the Sofia line.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. A gas-lamp-lit platform at night, uniformed officials checking papers along a sleeping-car corridor, breath visible in the cold air.

stops.name: Sofia
stops.note: The last mountain morning. Beyond the window the Balkan passes give way to tobacco fields, and the corridor talk turns, at last, to the sea.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. A Balkan station at first light, a dark-green express drawn up beneath wooded hills, a vendor with a tea urn on the platform.

stops.name: Istanbul (Sirkeci)
stops.note: The buffers of Sirkeci, a step from the Golden Horn — the end of the line and, this time, the end of the run. The board is lifted down for the last time and the name goes into memory.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Sirkeci terminus with its Ottoman-revival façade and stained-glass fanlights, a blue sleeping-car at the buffers, the Bosphorus bright beyond the doors. railway terminus platform at the buffers, Bosphorus bay water beyond.

hook: The through-carriage that carried Agatha Christie's cast and, a century before, the very first inaugural run — travelled one last time, Paris to the Bosphorus, before the name outlived the train.
